purpose: Bounded checkpoint handoff for benchmark-relevant agent-loop readiness signals without running the full benchmark harness.

benchmarkRelevantSignals.name: safeWorkspaceOperations
benchmarkRelevantSignals.expectedEvidence: Root-bounded read/write/list/mkdir behavior and stable path-escape errors are checked from ordinary Clasp code.

benchmarkRelevantSignals.name: subprocessVerifierExecution
benchmarkRelevantSignals.expectedEvidence: Workspace-confined argv subprocess execution reports stdout, stderr, exit status, timeout, cwd escape, and shell-string rejection data.

benchmarkRelevantSignals.name: structuredDiagnostics
benchmarkRelevantSignals.expectedEvidence: Native parser/checker diagnostics expose stable location, code, context, expected, and actual fields for verifier assertions.

benchmarkRelevantSignals.name: ordinaryProgramAgentLoop
benchmarkRelevantSignals.expectedEvidence: claspc run executes a Clasp builder/verifier loop that invokes Codex directly, writes durable status/events/artifacts, and runs a verifier command.

capabilityCoverage.name: ordinary_program_execution
capabilityCoverage.scope: ordinary Clasp agent-loop scenario via claspc run

capabilityCoverage.name: durable_native_substrate
capabilityCoverage.scope: status, event, and artifact persistence only; DAG edges, leases, approvals, and merge policy state are outside this bounded probe

capabilityCoverage.name: clasp_native_control_api
capabilityCoverage.scope: ordinary Clasp workspace and subprocess APIs used without compiler-level swarm commands

capabilityCoverage.name: orchestration_viability
capabilityCoverage.scope: combined file, process, diagnostic, and builder/verifier-loop readiness

capabilityCoverage.name: ergonomics
capabilityCoverage.scope: small ordinary Clasp wrappers and stable failure data for state-heavy code

capabilityCoverage.name: verification_gate
capabilityCoverage.scope: scenario-level focused checks for benchmark-facing ordinary-program and runtime behavior
